tasks.label: check-admin
args: $p=[Security.Principal.WindowsPrincipal][Security.Principal.WindowsIdentity]::GetCurrent(); if(-not $p.IsInRole([Security.Principal.WindowsBuiltInRole]'Administrator')){ Write-Host ''; Write-Host '================================================================' -ForegroundColor Red; Write-Host ' ERROR: VS Code is NOT running as Administrator.' -ForegroundColor Red; Write-Host '================================================================' -ForegroundColor Red; Write-Host ''; Write-Host ' This project writes its build output into the Cimatron Program' -ForegroundColor Red; Write-Host ' folder, which requires elevated privileges. Also make sure' -ForegroundColor Red; Write-Host ' Cimatron is closed so the previous DLL is not locked.' -ForegroundColor Red; Write-Host ''; Write-Host ' To restart VS Code as Administrator:' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Write-Host '   1. Close all VS Code windows.' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Write-Host '   2. Right-click the VS Code shortcut and choose Run as administrator.' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Write-Host '   3. Re-open this workspace folder.' -ForegroundColor Yellow; Write-Host ''; exit 1 } else { Write-Host 'Admin check passed.' -ForegroundColor Green }
